patterns_recognized: F1_xera_gap* without F3 >= 1000 produces redundant corroboration: validated 4+ consecutive suppressions through 7/17 (LAD@NYY F1*=1.61 no F3, CHW@TOR F1*=1.87 no F3, both lost). Starred SP sample means F1 and SP xERA draw from the same thin Statcast pool — not independent anchors.

GOLD CONFIRM at the 65% home-favorite boundary requires an F-signal beyond BvP OWNERs: 7/17 BAL@HOU (pick_prob=65.3%, two OWNERs Altuve+Alvarez vs Kremer, no F-signal) was a P&L loss 3-2. BvP career splits share informational content with p_model and cannot serve as sole independent corroboration.

HARD CAP 7 false-negatives accumulate on sub-0.04 bullpen gaps: KC@BAL 7/10 (+0.018 gap, BAL won 5-3) and 7/12 (+0.0189, BAL won 8-2) both won after CAP 7 fired. Today's MIA@MIL has +0.0185 — same problematic range; monitoring warranted.

Stage 2 high-delta direction reliable unless |pqi_diff| > 20 and PQI opposes: first failure 7/17 STL@ARI (f5_full_delta=0.186, PQI delta=-25.8 opposing Stage 2, STL won 5-4). Four-of-five streak otherwise holds — do not cite Stage 2 direction as soft confirmation when large PQI strongly opposes.

PIT @ CLE.reasoning: SKIP on three grounds: edge +2.46pp is below the 4pp valid floor; f5_full_delta=0.2691 (Stage 1 gives CLE 75.65% F5 while Stage 2 flips to PIT 51.3%); HARD CAP 10 fires at pick_prob=0.513 with no F-signal present. SP edge 0.81 (Jared Jones) and PQI delta=-8.1 are genuinely positive but cannot rescue CAP 10 without an F-signal anchor. Stage 2 direction toward PIT is consistent with the high-delta archive pattern but no stake qualifies without four dimensions.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 10 four-dimension rescue requires F-signal

MIN @ CHC.reasoning: D-grade with dual opposing signals: Taj Bradley (MIN) holds a 1.08 xERA advantage over Matthew Boyd (CHC), PQI delta=-10.8 opposes CHC, and edge is -8.89pp (HARD CAP 1 fires). pick_prob=0.502 is the slate's closest coin-flip. D-grade dual-opposing (SP against + PQI against) has been the July archive's most reliable SKIP indicator — validated repeatedly through 7/17. CONFIRM SKIP.
lessons_applied: D-grade dual-opposing SP+PQI = reliable SKIP (7/11 through 7/17 archive)

CHW @ TOR.reasoning: Two positive dimensions (PQI delta=+8.5, Stage agree delta=0.03) but no F-signal and no SP-xERA dimension in grade_reasons. HARD CAP 10 fires at pick_prob=0.530. Without an F-signal anchor, the two-dimension stack does not meet the CAP 10 four-dimension rescue threshold. Edge 4.73pp is at the valid-window floor but post-cap score is already 0. No live context justifies elevation. CONFIRM SKIP.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 10 rescue requires F+SP+PQI+Stage simultaneously — two dimensions insufficient
7/17 CHW@TOR DOWNGRADE validated (F1* no F3 lost 12-4)

CIN @ COL.reasoning: D-grade with dual opposing signals: Rhett Lowder (CIN) holds a 1.59 xERA advantage over Tomoyuki Sugano (COL), PQI delta=-9.8 opposes COL, edge +1.31pp is below the 4pp floor. Stage 1/2 agree (delta=0.00) is the sole positive dimension. Note: 7/17 flagged CIN@COL as the strongest DOWNGRADE false-negative (CIN won 7-2) — but today's pick is COL, the opposing side, with the same SP-against configuration that drove CIN's win. CONFIRM SKIP.
lessons_applied: 7/17 CIN@COL false-negative: CIN won, not COL — pick direction differs today

NYM @ PHI.reasoning: D-grade with structural SKIP on multiple gates: fair_prob=0.373 is below the 0.42 viability floor; Jesus Luzardo (PHI) holds a 1.26 xERA advantage over Sean Manaea (NYM); PQI delta=+25.4 opposes NYM; edge 16.46pp exceeds the 15pp ceiling. Market pricing NYM at 37.3% while model assigns 53.7% is a material calibration gap consistent with the hallucination-zone pattern (7/17 TEX@ATL, 7/17 WSH@OAK). Cannot verify live injury context. CONFIRM SKIP.

BAL @ HOU.reasoning: D-grade with dual opposing signals approaching the home-favorite scrutiny boundary: Trevor Rogers (BAL) holds a 0.93 xERA advantage over Spencer Arrighetti (HOU), PQI delta=-12.6 opposes HOU. pick_prob=0.644 is just below the >65% over-confidence zone. Yesterday's BAL@HOU (7/17) was the sole staked GOLD CONFIRM and lost 3-2 — same matchup, same pitcher pairing context, no F-signal. D-grade + dual-opposing + home-favorite boundary: CONFIRM SKIP.
lessons_applied: 7/17 BAL@HOU GOLD loss (BvP OWNERs insufficient without F-signal)

SD @ KC.reasoning: GOLD label overstates conviction: F3_swing_take_gap=613.5 is well below the 1000 meaningful threshold — per 7/11 postgame, sub-1000 F3 does not anchor a GOLD pick. Edge +0.16pp is trivially positive and outside the [4,15]pp valid window. HARD CAP 10 fires at pick_prob=0.519. F5_bullpen_gap=+1.44 is a soft positive; hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=-0.039 shows SD's bullpen marginally better (sub-0.04 meaningful threshold). Without a valid F3 anchor or in-range edge, GOLD is misleading. DOWNGRADE to SKIP.
lessons_applied: F3 < 1000 is not a meaningful anchor (7/11 PHI@DET: F3=328.8 correctly suppressed)
Edge outside [4,15]pp valid window blocks stake

STL @ ARI.reasoning: D-grade with dual opposing signals and negative edge: Dustin May (STL) holds a 0.68 xERA advantage over Brandon Pfaadt (ARI), PQI delta=-6.5 opposes ARI, edge -0.34pp (HARD CAP 1 fires). Yesterday's STL@ARI (7/17) noted Stage 2 high-delta direction failed when |pqi_diff| > 20 opposing — today's PQI opposition is moderate (6.5), well below that threshold, so that exception does not apply. D-grade + negative edge + dual-opposing: CONFIRM SKIP.
lessons_applied: 7/17 STL@ARI PQI-vs-Stage-2 lesson (|pqi|>20 threshold not met today)

TB @ BOS.reasoning: sp_savant_gate=HARD_VETO fires because Patrick Sandoval has only 65 Statcast pitches (needs 100+) — SP xERA is unreliable. Edge -1.21pp is negative (HARD CAP 1). HARD CAP 10 fires at pick_prob=0.519. The F-signal (+1) in grade_reasons draws partly on Sandoval's vetoed data pool, reducing its independence. PQI delta=-8.5 also opposes BOS. 7/17 TB@BOS G2 confirmed sp_savant_gate=HARD_VETO was appropriate. CONFIRM SKIP.

TEX @ ATL.reasoning: sp_savant_gate=HARD_VETO fires because Owen Murphy (ATL home SP) has only 67 Statcast pitches — SP data unreliable for the picked team's own starter. Edge -1.03pp triggers HARD CAP 1 (pre-cap score 3→1). HARD CAP 10 fires at pick_prob=0.518 (score 1→0). Pre-cap stack was positive (PQI +5.2, Stage agree delta=0.00, F-signal), but Murphy's gate plus negative edge block any rescue. 7/17 TEX@ATL saw ATL win 15-1 against model's ~50% with a similar market gap — different SPs today but same SKIP caution applies. CONFIRM SKIP.

MIA @ MIL.reasoning: Strong pre-cap stack (score=5: F3=3225.7, F5=+0.86, SP edge 0.41 agrees, PQI +7.2 confirms, Stage agree delta=0.02) reduced to no-stake by HARD CAP 7 (hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0185 >= 0) and HARD CAP 10 (pick_prob=0.549). Note: +0.0185 is well below the 0.040 meaningful threshold; the archive records CAP 7 false negatives at this exact gap level (KC@BAL 7/10 +0.018 BAL won 5-3, 7/12 +0.0189 BAL won 8-2). Cap authority is maintained — the four-dimension CAP 10 rescue exception does not extend to games where CAP 7 also fires.
lessons_applied: HARD CAP 7 + HARD CAP 10 stacking — both caps authoritative
CAP 10 rescue exception requires CAP 10 as sole blocker
CAP 7 sub-0.04 gap false-negative tracking (KC@BAL 7/10 and 7/12 same range)

SF @ SEA.reasoning: D-grade with dual opposing signals: Bryan Woo (SEA) holds a 0.45 xERA advantage over Logan Webb (SF), PQI delta=+7.1 opposes SF. Edge 8.09pp is within the valid window but D-grade correctly overrides tier eligibility. 7/17 SF@SEA (SF won 7-0 against all signals) was explicitly flagged as a variance outlier — 'do not extract a pattern from this win.' D-grade dual-opposing signals remain reliable SKIP indicators. CONFIRM SKIP.
lessons_applied: 7/17 SF@SEA variance-win warning — do not generalize

LAD @ NYY.reasoning: GOLD label on a D-grade pick (pre-cap score=-1): only F5_bullpen_gap=+0.98 supports NYY; SP edge 0.51 xERA AGAINST NYY (Emmet Sheehan LAD over Ryan Weathers NYY) and PQI delta=-11.0 opposing NYY pull the score negative. F5-only without F1 or F3 is an insufficient anchor per 7/12 SEA@TB (F1* no F3, 8-2 blowout loss) and 7/17 LAD@NYY (F1*+F5 no F3, DOWNGRADE confirmed correct). Rolling-win-rate guardrail already sets stake_mult=0 on GOLD. DOWNGRADE to SKIP.
lessons_applied: F5-only signal without F1/F3 insufficient for GOLD tier
7/17 LAD@NYY DOWNGRADE lesson (F1*+F5 no F3 — LAD won 2-1)

WSH @ OAK.reasoning: D-grade with severe dual opposing signals: J.T. Ginn (OAK) holds a 2.32 xERA advantage over Zack Littell (WSH), PQI delta=+34.8 opposes WSH (among the largest on the slate). Edge 16.08pp exceeds the 15pp ceiling. Market assigns WSH ~53.2% implied (fair_prob=0.468 for OAK) while model assigns 62.9% — a material calibration gap. Yesterday's WSH@OAK 23-4 blowout is non-transferable (different SPs today); the postgame noted 'direction correct, probability inflated' — same caution applies. CONFIRM SKIP.

DET @ LAA.reasoning: Most extreme SKIP on the slate: Tarik Skubal (DET) holds a 3.78 xERA advantage over Grayson Rodriguez (LAA) — largest SP gap of any matchup today. PQI delta=-38.3 opposing LAA is the slate's largest opposition. fair_prob=0.336 (market gives LAA only 33.6%, below the 0.42 viability floor). Stage 1 gives LAA only 19.25% F5 probability. Grade D, score=-4. Five-signal opposition analogous to 7/8 LAA@TEX (13-1 blowout) — LAA model direction should be treated as noise. CONFIRM SKIP.
lessons_applied: Five-signal opposition = direction is noise (7/8 LAA@TEX 13-1 pattern)
Largest SP gap + largest PQI opposition on slate

PIT @ CLE (G2).reasoning: Probable SP for PIT (game 2) not yet announced. Pipeline correctly withholds a directional call until SP data clears the 100+ Statcast pitch minimum. No evaluation possible. Game 1 PIT@CLE is tracked separately above.
lessons_applied: PENDING_SP_DATA non-directional hold per pipeline gate
